title: trifolium beckwithii
intro: Trifolium beckwithii, also known as Beckwith's clover, is a rare species of flowering plant in the pea family Fabaceae. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of fever and as an anti-inflammatory agent. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and promoting digestion. Trifolium beckwithii can be prepared in herbal form, often used as a tea or infusion, or dried for use in capsules or tinctures.
